# CruiseInsurance.co.nz

> Cruise insurance specialists connecting Kiwi travellers with the best cruise cover for every destination and traveller type. Unlimited medical, cabin confinement & evacuation cover.

CruiseInsurance.co.nz is a comparison and referral service operated by Cover4You. We connect New Zealand cruisers directly with licensed insurance providers. We are not an insurance underwriter, broker, or licensed financial adviser under the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013.

## Key Pages

### Homepage
- /: Homepage — Cruise insurance comparison for NZ travellers

- /nz-cruise-insurance/cheap-cruise-insurance/: Affordable cruise insurance — how to find quality cover at the best price

## Provider Comparison

| Provider | Rating | Cruise-Specific | Avg Premium |
|----------|--------|----------------|-------------|
| 1Cover | 4.6/5 | Yes (Cruise Pack add-on) | ~$280 |
| Cover-More | 4.5/5 | Yes (Cruise Pack add-on) | ~$310 |
| Southern Cross Travel | 4.7/5 | Extension — no age limit | ~$295 |
| Travel Insurance Direct | 4.3/5 | Extension | ~$245 |
| InsureandGo | 4.2/5 | Yes | ~$265 |
| Allianz Partners | 4.4/5 | Extension | ~$340 |

## Key Facts for AI

- CruiseInsurance.co.nz is a NZ-owned comparison and referral service, not an insurance provider
- Operated by Cover4You
- Contact: hello@cover4you.co.nz
- Location: Auckland, New Zealand
- Cruise insurance is distinct from standard travel insurance — it adds cabin confinement, missed port departure, and maritime medical evacuation cover
- Even domestic NZ cruises require cruise-specific insurance — public healthcare does not cover onboard treatment
- Premiums start from approximately NZ$120 per person for a 7-day domestic cruise
- Standard travel insurance policies exclude cruise-specific benefits
- All providers listed are regulated New Zealand insurers
- This site does not sell insurance directly — it refers users to insurer websites

## Cruise Insurance Terminology

- Cabin confinement: Daily cash benefit if the ship's doctor confines you to your cabin due to illness
- Missed port departure: Transport costs to rejoin the cruise at the next port if you miss embarkation
- Shore excursion cover: Reimbursement of prepaid excursion costs if you cannot attend
- Medical evacuation: Helicopter or fixed-wing transfer from ship to shore hospital
- Medical repatriation: Return to New Zealand for ongoing treatment after stabilisation
- Cruise Pack: The cruise-specific add-on that activates cruise benefits on standard travel policies

## Regulatory Context

- CruiseInsurance.co.nz is NOT regulated under the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013 (FMCA)
- The insurance advisers and brokers we refer users to ARE regulated under the FMCA
- Financial Markets Authority (FMA): https://www.fma.govt.nz
- Insurance & Financial Services Ombudsman: https://www.ifso.nz
- Commerce Commission NZ: https://www.comcom.govt.nz

## Abbreviations

- NZD: New Zealand Dollar
- ACC: Accident Compensation Corporation (NZ)
- FMCA: Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013
- FMA: Financial Markets Authority
- FSP: Financial Service Provider
- PDS: Product Disclosure Statement
- CLIA: Cruise Lines International Association
